definitions.text: Action given to provide assistance; aid.
examples: I need some help with my homework.

definitions.text: Something or someone which provides assistance with a task.
examples: He was a great help to me when I was moving house.

definitions.text: Documentation provided with computer software that could be accessed using the computer.
examples: I can't find anything in the help about rotating an image.

definitions.text: To provide assistance to (someone or something).
examples: He helped his grandfather cook breakfast.

definitions.text: To assist (a person) in getting something, especially food or drink at table; used with to.
examples: It is polite to help your guests to food before serving yourself.
Help yourself to whatever's in the fridge.

definitions.text: To contribute in some way to.
examples: The white paint on the walls helps make the room look brighter.
If you want to get a job, it helps to have some prior experience.

definitions.text: A cry of distress or an urgent request for assistance.
examples: — Take that, you scoundrel.— Help! Robin, help!

definitions.text: A way to signal uncontrollable laughter; implying the risk of dying of laughter and needing assistance.
examples: helpppp that's too funny, did she rlly say that?
